sn.s: S'Schwiizerdütsch het e ganz eigeni Melodie.
sn.e: Swiss German has its very own melody.

sn.s: D'Sätz gönd ufe und abe, fascht wie es Lied.
sn.e: Sentences go up and down, almost like a song.

sn.s: Am Schluss vom Satz gaht d'Stimm oft chli ufe.
sn.e: At the end of a sentence the voice often rises a little.

sn.s: Das git em Rede öppis Fröhlichs, fascht Frögends.
sn.e: That gives the speech something cheerful, almost questioning.

sn.s: Mer cha d'Wörter wüsse und doch falsch töne.
sn.e: You can know the words and still sound wrong.
The melody is the hardest part, not the vocabulary.

sn.s: Ich lose de Lüt zue und mach d'Betoonig nah.
sn.e: I listen to people and imitate the intonation.

sn.s: Im Tram, im Lade, überall ghör ich gnau häre.
sn.e: On the tram, in the shop, everywhere I listen closely.

sn.s: Mängisch red ich für mich säuber und probier de Klang.
sn.e: Sometimes I talk to myself and try out the sound.

sn.s: Es tönt komisch, aber es hilft würkli.
sn.e: It sounds funny, but it really helps.

sn.s: Langsam chunnt d'Melodie i mini eigeni Stimm.
sn.e: Slowly the melody is coming into my own voice.

sn.s: Öpper het gseit, ich red scho ganz natürli.
sn.e: Someone said I already speak quite naturally.

sn.s: S'Ghör isch wichtiger gsii als s'Buech.
sn.e: The ear was more important than the book.

sn.s: Wer d'Melodie het, de ghört dezue.
